author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
joga-pīṭhopari sthita, aṣṭasakhī⋅suveṣṭita,
vṛndāraṇye kadamba⋅kānane
rādhāsaha vaṁśī-dhārī, viśvajana⋅cittahārī,
prāṇa mora tā̐hāra caraṇe

sakhī⋅ājñāmata kari dohāra sevana
pālya-dāsī sadā bhāvi dohāra caraṇa

kabhu kṛpā kari’, mama hasta dhari’,
madhura vacana bale
tāmbūla loiyā, khāya duijane,
mālā laya kutūhale

adarśana haya kakhana ki chale
nā dekhiyā dohe hiyā mora jvale

jekhāne sekhāne, thākuka du’jane,
āmi ta’ caraṇa⋅dāsī
milane ānanda, virahe jātanā,
sakala samāna vāsi

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa prāṇa mora jīvane maraṇe
more rākhi’ māri’ sukhe thākuka du’jane

bhakativinoda, āna nāhi jāne,
paḍi’ nijasakhī⋅pāya
rādhikāra gaṇe, thākiyā satata,
jugala⋅caraṇa cāya
en_translation:
Situated on the altar of union, surrounded by the eight chief companions in the kadamba groves of Vṛndāvana, is the flute-bearer with Rādhā, the captivator of everyone's heart. My very life is at His feet.

According to the companions' command, I serve them both. As a nurtured maidservant, I always meditate on Their feet.

Sometimes, by Their grace, They take my hand and speak sweet words. Taking betel, They both eat, and they take garlands with eagerness.

When they disappear by some pretense, my heart burns at not seeing them.

Wherever They may be, let them remain. I am indeed a maidservant of Their feet. In union, there is joy; in separation, there is distress—I consider all these feelings to be equal.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are my life in both life and death. Whether protecting me or killing me, let them remain happy.

Bhaktivinoda knows nothing else. Falling at the feet of his own companions and always remaining among Rādhā's associates, he desires the feet of the Divine Couple.
